description: Transitive Dependents & Content-Level Diff Analysis - extend query_dependencies with cycle-safe BFS up to depth=3, and rewrite map_diff_impact to flag only symbols whose line ranges intersect changed hunks.

userStories.title: Build cycle-safe BFS core for transitive dependents
userStories.description: As a graph-layer caller, I want a reusable BFS that returns dependents up to depth=N over a one-time reverse-adjacency map, so that all callers (sync, batch, future tools) share the same semantics and perf profile.
acceptanceCriteria: Add pub fn compute_transitive_dependents(target, reverse, depth) -> TransitiveResult in crates/seshat-graph/src/dependencies.rs
Add pub fn build_reverse_adjacency(files, internal_names, suffix_index) -> HashMap<String, Vec<ReverseEdge>> as a single O(N×D) pass
BFS uses visited: HashSet<String> keyed by file path; cycle a→b→a terminates without re-enqueue
Self-edges skipped at edge-build time
MAX_DEPENDENTS = 500 cap; on overflow truncated=true; direct entries enumerated before transitive so they are preserved across capping
Extract shared helper resolve_import_to_known_path from import_resolves_to_target (dependencies.rs:703-792); reverse-edge keys go through it

Unit tests pass: transitive_depth_2_includes_2nd_order, transitive_depth_3_includes_3rd_order, transitive_cycle_a_b_a_terminates, transitive_diamond_visits_each_node_once, transitive_truncation_caps_at_max_dependents
cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
cargo check passes
userStories.notes: Implemented in crates/seshat-graph/src/dependencies.rs: pub const MAX_DEPENDENTS=500; pub struct ReverseEdge; pub struct TransitiveResult; pub struct SuffixIndex (made pub); DependentEntry extended with depth+via (#[serde(default)]); pub fn build_reverse_adjacency; pub fn compute_transitive_dependents (cycle-safe BFS, lex-deterministic, MAX_DEPENDENTS cap, direct-first); shared package_boundary_ok helper used by both import_resolves_to_target and the new pub(crate) resolve_import_to_known_path. Five required unit tests pass; full seshat-graph test suite (237 tests) and workspace clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean.

userStories.title: Add depth parameter to query_dependencies graph-layer API
userStories.description: As a graph-layer caller, I want to opt into transitive results via a depth parameter so existing callsites can preserve direct-only semantics by passing depth=1.
acceptanceCriteria: Add pub struct QueryDependenciesOptions { pub depth: u32 } with Default::default() returning depth=1
query_dependencies and query_dependencies_batch signatures gain trailing opts: QueryDependenciesOptions parameter
query_dependencies_batch builds reverse-adjacency exactly once across the whole IR (amortized across all targets)
DependentEntry extended with depth: u32 and via: Vec<String> (full file paths, intermediate only); import_names and line populated only when depth==1, otherwise vec![] and 0
DependencyData extended with transitive_dependent_count: usize and requested_depth: u32
blast_radius is computed from direct count only (preserves existing thresholds)
Validation: depth not in [1, MAX_TRANSITIVE_DEPTH=10] returns GraphError::InvalidInput
Diamond via tie-break: lexicographic on the joined path string (deterministic across runs)
All existing callsites in crates/seshat-graph/src/validate_approach.rs and crates/seshat-graph/src/diff_impact.rs:378 updated to pass QueryDependenciesOptions { depth: 1 }
All existing seshat-graph tests pass with no semantic regression
cargo check passes
userStories.notes: Implemented in crates/seshat-graph/src/dependencies.rs. Added pub const MAX_TRANSITIVE_DEPTH=10, pub struct QueryDependenciesOptions { depth: u32 } with Default::default() returning depth=1. DependencyData gained transitive_dependent_count: usize and requested_depth: u32 (both #[serde(default)] for envelope forward-compat). query_dependencies and query_dependencies_batch take a trailing QueryDependenciesOptions; depth=1 takes the existing build_dependents fast path (no semantic regression), depth>=2 builds reverse-adjacency once and BFS over it. For _batch with depth>1 the reverse-adjacency map is built exactly once across the whole IR and shared across targets. blast_radius is classified from the direct count only — existing thresholds preserved. validate_depth() rejects depth=0 or depth>MAX_TRANSITIVE_DEPTH with GraphError::InvalidInput before any IR load. Callers updated: validate_approach.rs::enrich_used_by, diff_impact.rs:378 query_dependencies_batch site, and seshat-mcp tools/query_dependencies.rs::handle (the MCP tool still hard-wires depth=1 — wiring up tool-level depth is US-003). All 237 seshat-graph tests pass; full workspace tests pass (one flaky serve_guardrails test passes when re-run); cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.

userStories.title: Expose depth parameter on query_dependencies MCP tool
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to request transitive depth via the MCP tool input so I can see 2nd/3rd-order ripple without a separate tool.
acceptanceCriteria: QueryDependenciesRequest in crates/seshat-mcp/src/tools/query_dependencies.rs:17-51 gains pub depth: Option<u32> with #[schemars(description = ...)]
Handler maps req.depth.unwrap_or(DEFAULT_TRANSITIVE_DEPTH=3) to QueryDependenciesOptions
Validation: depth=0 or depth>10 returns INVALID_INPUT with suggestion 'Use depth between 1 and 10'
Tool description in crates/seshat-mcp/src/server.rs:545 updated to mention depth, default value, and max value
Response JSON includes data.transitive_dependent_count, data.requested_depth, and per-entry depth/via/import_names/line fields

Integration tests pass: query_dependencies_tool_default_depth_returns_transitive, query_dependencies_tool_depth_one_returns_direct_only, query_dependencies_tool_depth_zero_returns_invalid_input, query_dependencies_tool_depth_above_max_returns_invalid_input
cargo check passes
userStories.notes: Implemented in crates/seshat-mcp/src/tools/query_dependencies.rs and crates/seshat-mcp/src/server.rs:545. Added pub const DEFAULT_TRANSITIVE_DEPTH: u32 = 3 and pub depth: Option<u32> on QueryDependenciesRequest (with #[schemars(description=...)] mentioning the 1..=10 range and default of 3). Handler resolves req.depth.unwrap_or(DEFAULT_TRANSITIVE_DEPTH) into seshat_graph::QueryDependenciesOptions { depth }; validates depth==0 || depth>MAX_TRANSITIVE_DEPTH up-front and returns INVALID_INPUT with the explicit suggestion 'Use depth between 1 and 10' (rather than letting GraphError::InvalidInput's generic suggestion fire). The #[tool(description=...)] on server.rs:545 was rewritten to mention the new depth parameter, default value, and max value. Existing test callsites in both files updated to pass depth: None. Four new integration tests added in server.rs (sample_dependency_chain helper builds utils←handler←main): query_dependencies_tool_default_depth_returns_transitive (depth=None surfaces handler.rs at depth=1 plus main.rs at depth=2 with via=[handler.rs]), query_dependencies_tool_depth_one_returns_direct_only (depth=Some(1) excludes main.rs), query_dependencies_tool_depth_zero_returns_invalid_input (canonical suggestion text), query_dependencies_tool_depth_above_max_returns_invalid_input. Full workspace cargo test passes; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.

userStories.title: Surface transitive count and depth in call-logger summary
userStories.description: As an operator reading call logs, I want the transitive count and effective depth surfaced in the per-call summary so I can debug expensive queries.
acceptanceCriteria: crates/seshat-mcp/src/call_logger_keys.rs::dependencies adds DATA_TRANSITIVE_DEPENDENT_COUNT and DATA_REQUESTED_DEPTH constants
dependencies_result() in crates/seshat-mcp/src/call_logger.rs includes both new fields in the summary JSON
Existing call-logger tests are updated to assert on the new fields and pass
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Add performance guard test for transitive query_dependencies
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want an explicit perf budget test so the 1s P95 target for query_dependencies at depth=3 is verifiable.
acceptanceCriteria: New crates/seshat-graph/tests/transitive_perf.rs gated with #[ignore] by default
Test builds 3000 synthetic IR files in a 3-level fan-out tree and runs query_dependencies at depth=3
Asserts wall-clock duration < 500 ms (2× headroom on the 1s MCP P95 target)
Runnable via: cargo test -p seshat-graph --test transitive_perf -- --ignored --nocapture
Running it with --ignored on dev hardware passes
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Add end_line to Export and TypeDef IR structs (schema v8)
userStories.description: As an IR consumer, I want every public symbol to carry both line and end_line so hunk intersection can use the same logic across all symbol kinds.
acceptanceCriteria: Add pub end_line: usize to Export and TypeDef in crates/seshat-core/src/ir.rs:126-172
IR_SCHEMA_VERSION in crates/seshat-storage/src/ir_serialization.rs:29 bumped from 7 to 8 (StaleIR detection auto-triggers re-scan; no migration script)
All construction sites in crates/seshat-scanner/src/parser/{rust,typescript,javascript,python}_parser.rs populate end_line via node.end_position().row + 1 (matching the existing Function pattern)
For single-line statements (pub use foo::*;, export { Foo };, type Alias = X;), end_line == line
Round-trip serialization tests updated and a fresh scan produces schema version 8
All existing seshat-core, seshat-storage, and seshat-scanner tests pass
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Add hunk extraction primitive using gix::diff::blob
userStories.description: As a diff consumer, I want a self-contained Vec<Hunk> for any pair of file blobs, using the algorithm git uses by default (Histogram).
acceptanceCriteria: Add types LineRange { start, end } (1-based half-open) and Hunk { old: LineRange, new: LineRange } in crates/seshat-graph/src/diff_impact.rs
Add Hunk::ALL constant covering an entire file (binary/oversized fallback)
Add helpers: is_pure_insertion, is_pure_deletion, touches_new_line(line)
Add pub fn diff_blobs_to_hunks(old: &[u8], new: &[u8]) -> Vec<Hunk> using gix::diff::blob with the Histogram algorithm
8 unit tests pass: no-change, single-replacement, pure-insertion at top, pure-insertion at bottom, pure-insertion in middle, pure-deletion, multi-hunk, empty-old or empty-new
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Blob-aware change enumeration for diff_impact
userStories.description: As compute_affected_symbols, I want each changed file accompanied by its base_blob_id and index_blob_id so I can read both sides for diffing.
acceptanceCriteria: Add struct ChangedFileWithBlobs { path, status, base_blob_id: Option<gix::ObjectId>, index_blob_id: Option<gix::ObjectId> }
Refactor get_changed_files() (crates/seshat-graph/src/diff_impact.rs:183-348) to delegate to a new enumerate_changes_with_blobs(); old function becomes a thin wrapper if still needed
Add read_blob_pair(repo, repo_path, rel_path, base_blob_id, index_blob_id, staged_only) -> Result<Option<(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>)>>; returns None for binary or oversized so callers fall back to Hunk::ALL
is_binary_blob heuristic: NUL byte in first 8 KiB
MAX_DIFF_FILE_SIZE = 5 * 1024 * 1024 cap enforced
Unit tests pass: staged-Deleted produces no index_blob_id, Added produces no base_blob_id, binary blob makes read_blob_pair return None
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Rewrite compute_affected_symbols to hunk-level granularity
userStories.description: As an AI agent calling map_diff_impact, I want only the symbols whose line ranges actually intersect changed hunks - not every public symbol in the file.
acceptanceCriteria: crates/seshat-graph/src/diff_impact.rs:355-451 rewritten: per-changed-file, get hunks via read_blob_pair + diff_blobs_to_hunks, then symbol_intersects_hunks(line, end_line, hunks) -> Vec<Hunk> selects affected symbols

AffectedSymbol extended (BREAKING - pre-1.0) with changed_lines: Vec<(usize, usize)> (intersecting hunk ranges) and direct_dependent_count: usize; dependent_count semantically becomes the transitive count
query_dependencies_batch callsite at crates/seshat-graph/src/diff_impact.rs:378 switches to QueryDependenciesOptions { depth: DEFAULT_TRANSITIVE_DEPTH }
blast_radius is re-classified using the new transitive count
Modified files: hunks computed against HEAD blob (or index blob if staged_only, or base blob if base param given)
Added files: every symbol affected with changed_lines = [(line, end_line)] per symbol
Deleted files: file reported in changed_files with no per-symbol entries (V1 limitation: old IR not loaded)
Untracked / Conflicted / binary / oversized: fall back to Hunk::ALL (preserves current behavior)

Integration tests pass: single_hunk_in_function_body_flags_only_that_function, multi_hunk_flags_each_intersecting_symbol, hunk_between_symbols_flags_neither, added_file_flags_all_symbols_with_their_ranges, deleted_file_reports_no_symbols_only_status, binary_modified_file_falls_back_to_hunk_all, transitive_dependent_count_uses_depth_3
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Surface content-level granularity in map_diff_impact MCP wiring and logs
userStories.description: As an AI agent reading the map_diff_impact response, I want next_steps and call logs to surface the new content-level granularity (changed_lines, direct vs transitive counts, total hunks).
acceptanceCriteria: crates/seshat-mcp/src/tools/diff_impact.rs:97-176 generate_next_steps mentions changed_lines and shows direct_dependent_count separately from transitive (e.g. 'foo touched at lines 42-58 with 12 transitive (4 direct) dependents in ...')
crates/seshat-mcp/src/call_logger.rs::diff_impact_result adds total_hunks to the summary JSON
Existing next_steps_* MCP-handler tests updated for new field defaults and pass
cargo check passes

userStories.title: Add criterion bench for map_diff_impact perf budget
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a perf bench locking the 1s P95 budget on a representative diff so regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: New crates/seshat-graph/benches/diff_impact_bench.rs using criterion
Bench drives a 50-file synthetic diff with a mix of single-hunk and multi-hunk modifications
Asserts (or documents as a CI gate) median wall-clock < 1000 ms on dev hardware
Runnable via cargo bench -p seshat-graph --bench diff_impact_bench
cargo check passes
